2604.19440.title: What Makes an LLM a Good Optimizer? A Trajectory Analysis of LLM-Guided Evolutionary Search
2604.19440.paper_summary: Recent work has demonstrated the promise of orchestrating large language models (LLMs) within evolutionary and agentic optimization systems. However, the mechanisms driving these optimization gains remain poorly understood. In this work, we present a large-scale study of LLM-guided evolutionary search, collecting optimization trajectories for 15 LLMs across 8 tasks. Although zero-shot problem-solving ability correlates with final optimization outcomes, it explains only part of the variance: models with similar initial capability often induce dramatically different search trajectories and outcomes. By analyzing these trajectories, we find that strong LLM optimizers behave as local refiners, producing frequent incremental improvements while progressively localizing the search in semantic space. Conversely, weaker optimizers exhibit large semantic drift, with sporadic breakthroughs followed by stagnation. Notably, various measures of solution novelty do not predict final performance; novelty is beneficial only when the search remains sufficiently localized around high-performing regions of the solution space. Our results highlight the importance of trajectory analysis for understanding and improving LLM-based optimization systems and provide actionable insights for their design and training.
2604.19440.paper_summary_zh: 最近的研究顯示，在進化和代理優化系統中協調大型語言模型（LLMs）的潛力。然而，驅動這些優化增益的機制仍然不甚了解。在這項工作中，我們呈現了一項大規模的 LLM 指導進化搜索研究，收集了 15 個 LLM 在 8 個任務中的優化軌跡。儘管零-shot 問題解決能力與最終優化結果相關，但它僅解釋了部分變異：具有相似初始能力的模型往往會產生截然不同的搜索軌跡和結果。通過分析這些軌跡，我們發現強大的 LLM 優化器表現為局部精煉者，產生頻繁的增量改進，同時逐步在語義空間中本地化搜索。相反，較弱的優化器則表現出較大的語義漂移，偶爾出現突破，隨後陷入停滯。值得注意的是，各種解決方案新穎性的衡量標準並不能預測最終表現；新穎性只有在搜索足夠集中於高性能解決方案空間的區域時才是有益的。我們的結果突顯了軌跡分析在理解和改善基於 LLM 的優化系統中的重要性，並為其設計和訓練提供了可行的見解。
2604.19440.author: Xinhao Zhang et.al.
2604.19440.authors: Xinhao Zhang, Xi Chen, François Portet, Maxime Peyrard
